title: Decadent Molten Mexican Beans Frijoles Charros
description: A pescatarian-friendly, molten Mexican dish featuring Thai green curry paste, with a mustardy flavor and cozy Oaxacan inspiration, served in a tajine clay vessel.
instructions: Rinse the dried pinto beans and soak them in water for at least 8 hours.
Drain and rinse the soaked beans, then transfer them to a large pot with 4 cups of water.
Bring the water to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 1 hour, or until the beans are tender.
In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat, then add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic, Thai green curry paste, ground cumin, smoked paprika, and salt to the skillet, and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Stir in the diced tomatoes and fish broth, then bring the mixture to a simmer.
Add the cooked pinto beans to the skillet, then stir to combine.
Transfer the bean mixture to a tajine clay vessel, then bake in a preheated oven at 375 degrees Fahrenheit for 20 minutes, or until the beans are molten and bubbly.
Remove the vessel from the oven, then let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
